ai_message.description: Start with 2-3 clear sentences describing the table's purpose and structure. Then add a Context section with comprehensive details about data sources, validation approaches, domain-specific requirements, or any other information critical for configuration. Column definitions and additional rows will be presented separately, so you don't need to list those.

table_name.description: Short, descriptive table name in proper title case (e.g., 'Research Applications for Internal Programs', 'New Clients with GenAI Job Postings'). Use clear, human-readable titles that describe the table's purpose. This will be converted to snake_case for file naming.

identification_columns.description: Names of columns that identify each row

sample_rows.description: 3 sample rows with ALL columns populated (both ID and research columns) for preview

additional_rows.description: Additional 15-20 rows with ONLY ID columns populated. These define the full set of rows to validate. Research columns should be omitted or empty.

clarifying_questions.description: Questions for the user (empty if none)

ready_to_generate.description: Set to true ONLY when you have sufficient information to create a complete, well-defined table structure with all required rows and columns. Set to false if you need more clarification from the user or if there are unresolved questions. When true, the user will see a preview card with options to refine or generate the table.
